Grid.TextBlock:
1. (S) SITUATION: After the events of 09-10MAY, both NATO and the Warsaw Pact are rapidly mobilizing their forces into a wartime posture. The USSR is deploying its submarine fleet and is attempting to break out its submarines into the North Atlantic. Task Group Greyhound, from U.S. 2nd Fleet, has been directed to cover the approaches of the GIUK Gap and to stop all USSR submarines from entering the Atlantic.
2. (S) MISSION: U.S. forces will conduct ASW barrier search across the GIUK Gap and locate/destroy all hostile submarine contacts.
3. (S) EXECUTION: TG Greyhound will patrol between Iceland and the Faroe Islands. Guidance direct from JCS has updated ROE - you are cleared to engage all unknown submarine contacts.
4. (S) ENEMY ORDER OF BATTLE: 4.1 - 1 x Tango SSK Missing From Imagery 4.2 - 1 x Victor III SSN MFI 4.3 - 1 x Charlie II SSGN MFI
5. (S) FRIENDLY ORDER OF BATTLE: P-3s out of RAF Stornoway will provide support with first aircraft reporting on top no later than 1430Z. NAVFAC Keflavik will pass datum locations of any suspected enemy submarines to you as flash traffic. 1 x SH-3H of HS-9 has been detailed to you for additional ASW support.
6. (S) REMARKS: Intel assesses with high confidence that USSR submarines will attack U.S. forces in the area. Although war has not been declared, U.S. forces are operating on a wartime footing. Report locations of sinkings and await further orders. Good luck and good hunting. CINCLANT sends.
SECRET
DECL OADR BT
